zh.lifeSummary: 當William 'the Junior' de Peverell II於1080年1月5日出生在英國諾廷加姆爾諾廷加姆時，他的父親Sir William de Peverel31歲，母親Adeline20歲。他和Avice de Lancaster至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1147年5月16日在英國米德萊可享年67歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Peverell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Peverall
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Old French Peurel (Latin Piperellus), a diminutive of Old French pevre ‘pepper’ (Anglo-Norman French peivere, Latin piper), originally perhaps used as a nickname on the Continent but used as a personal name in Anglo-Norman England.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: 'the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Junior'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the common nickname used to distinguish a son from his father or for any young male, used occasionally since the early 20th century as an independent given name, mainly in the United States.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William 'the Junior' de Peverell II was born on 5 January 1080, in Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Sir William de Peverel, was 31 and his mother, Adeline, was 20. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Avice de Lancaster. He died on 16 May 1147, in Middlesex, England, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William 'the Junior' de Peverell II est né le 5 janvier 1080, à Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Peverel, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Adeline, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Avice de Lancaster. Il est décédé le 16 mai 1147, à Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William 'the Junior' de Peverell II(이)가 1080년 1월 5일 영국 노틴그함실르 노틴그함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de Peverel(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Adeline(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Avice de Lancaster(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1147년 5월 16일 영국 미들레세스에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.
